name: Highway Alliance
description_short: Highway Alliance Credit Union is a Missouri-based credit union serving MODOT, Highway Patrol, and families since 1958, offering checking, savings, loans, and Visa cards.
description_long: Highway Alliance Credit Union was established in 1958 and has been serving members in Missouri for over 60 years, with a specific focus on employees of the Missouri Department of Transportation (MODOT), the Highway Patrol, and their families. The credit union is headquartered in Jefferson City, Missouri, and operates as a member-owned financial cooperative regulated by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration). The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including deposit accounts (General Share Accounts for savings, Share Draft checking accounts, and Super Share Accounts), certificates of deposit, and a Christmas Club savings product. On the lending side, they provide signature lines of credit, share-secured loans, collateral loans, and specialized lending products like lawnmower and small farm equipment loans. They also offer home loans with calculators and current rate information available online. Members can apply for Visa credit cards, and the credit union provides gap insurance and extended warranty products on certain loans. Highway Alliance distinguishes itself through its niche membership focus on government employees (MODOT and Highway Patrol) and their families, creating a specialized community-oriented financial institution rather than a broad-based bank. The credit union emphasizes convenience through modern digital services including online banking, bill pay, and a recently launched mobile app. They offer member-friendly features such as skip-a-pay loan options and payroll deduction programs. Their 24/7 lost or stolen debit card hotline and accessibility commitment (screen reader support) demonstrate attention to member service. As a credit union, Highway Alliance operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model, which typically results in lower fees and better loan rates compared to traditional banks. However, membership appears restricted to eligible groups (MODOT, Highway Patrol, and families), limiting accessibility to the general public. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Lawnmower and small farm equipment loans
Gap insurance and extended warranty products
pros: Member-owned credit union structure typically offers lower fees and competitive loan rates
24/7 lost or stolen debit card support via dedicated hotline (888) 263-3370
Extended service hours with drive-up access until 5:00 pm weekdays
Diverse loan products including specialized lawnmower/farm equipment financing and home loans
Mobile app available for digital banking and bill pay convenience
NCUA-insured deposits providing federal protection up to $250,000
Skip-a-pay loan options offering payment flexibility during hardship
cons: Membership eligibility restricted to MODOT employees, Highway Patrol, and families—not open to general public
Limited lobby hours (8:00 am-4:30 pm) Monday-Friday only with no weekend service
Website has technical issues with broken links (404 errors on member services pages)
No mention of online account opening or digital-only membership options
Limited branch presence compared to national banks—single Jefferson City location
best_for: Missouri MODOT employees and their family members seeking community-focused banking
Missouri Highway Patrol personnel needing member-owned credit union benefits
Individuals with property or farm equipment interested in specialized collateral loans
Borrowers seeking home financing with local relationship-based lending
diagnosis: Highway Alliance Credit Union is best for eligible Missouri government employees (MODOT and Highway Patrol staff and their families) seeking a member-owned financial institution with competitive rates and personalized service. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to specific employee groups—this is not an open-enrollment credit union available to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Highway Alliance Credit Union is a Missouri-based union serving MODOT, Highway Patrol, and families since 1958 with checking, savings, and loans.
